title: Persistence Test Strategy for Backend Services
content: This persistence test defines a robust method to verify that backend data remains correct, durable, and recoverable across normal operations and failures. Objective: ensure create, update, delete, and read flows preserve integrity under concurrency, restarts, and partial outages. Test dataset: include valid records, boundary-size payloads, unicode text, null/optional fields, and relational references. Functional persistence checks: confirm writes are durable after commit, reads are consistent with expected isolation level, updates are atomic, and deletes respect soft/hard delete policy. Transaction checks: validate rollback on mid-transaction failure, prevent partial writes, and confirm idempotency for retried requests with the same idempotency key. Concurrency checks: run parallel writers/readers against the same entities, verify no lost updates, and enforce optimistic/pessimistic lock behavior as designed. Restart resilience: perform controlled service restart and database restart, then confirm all acknowledged writes are still present and indexes remain healthy. Crash/failure scenarios: simulate process kill during write, network interruption, and storage latency spikes; verify recovery path and consistency after reconciliation jobs. Schema migration checks: run forward migration, validate old and new records, execute rollback rehearsal, and ensure zero data truncation for expanded fields. Backup and restore validation: take snapshot backup, restore into clean environment, compare row counts and sampled checksums, and verify application-level readability. Performance guardrails: measure write/read latency p95 and p99 under load, monitor queue depth, and detect persistence degradation before SLO breach. Observability requirements: emit structured audit logs for mutations, include correlation IDs, and track persistence error rates with alert thresholds. Exit criteria: all critical test cases pass, no data integrity violations, recovery drills are documented, and unresolved issues are triaged with owners and deadlines.
